codeReview.summary: This rerun fixes the two original forced-`ACTIVE` regressions by centralizing preserved-status resolution in `DealerProvisioningSupport` and applying it from both `DealerService.createDealer` and `AdminUserService.createDealerForUser`. It also adds a portal interceptor that correctly fences non-active dealer sessions to the intended read-only `/api/v1/dealer-portal/**` finance routes. However, the effective HEAD still leaves one dealer-facing write surface open: non-active dealer sessions can POST `/api/v1/credit/limit-requests`, so the packet still does not satisfy the mission contract that non-active dealer login access be finance-read-only only until explicit reactivation.

issues.description: `createRequest` still grants `ROLE_DEALER` access on `/api/v1/credit/limit-requests`, and the dealer branch at lines 50-57 resolves the caller through `dealerPortalService.getCurrentDealer()`. Because `DealerPortalService` now intentionally accepts `ON_HOLD`/`SUSPENDED`/`BLOCKED` dealers so finance reads keep working, a non-active dealer session can still create credit-limit requests through this non-finance POST endpoint. The new `DealerPortalFinanceReadOnlyInterceptor` only guards `/api/v1/dealer-portal/**`, so the fix seals the portal namespace but not the full non-active dealer login surface required by VAL-PARTNER-037 and mission AGENTS.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared-state guidance frames the restriction mostly as blocking broader `dealer-portal` access, but dealer-capable routes are not fully namespaced under `/api/v1/dealer-portal/**`. That makes path-prefix-only hardening easy to misapply.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission `AGENTS.md:34` says non-active dealer login access must be finance read-only only while broader dealer-portal access stays blocked, yet `erp-domain/src/main/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/modules/sales/controller/CreditLimitRequestController.java:46-57` still exposes a dealer-role POST outside the dealer-portal prefix.

summary: Fail: the rerun fixes both prior forced-reactivation bugs and correctly narrows non-active dealer access inside `/api/v1/dealer-portal/**`, but it still leaves `/api/v1/credit/limit-requests` writable to non-active dealer sessions, so finance-read-only-only access is not fully enforced.
